card.intuition: A modern CSS feature that allows you to style elements based on the size of their parent container rather than the size of the whole browser viewport.
card.analogy: Self-aware clothes: a shirt that turns into a jacket if you step into a cold 'room' (container), regardless of whether it's winter or summer in the 'rest of the world' (the viewport).
when_to_use: Creating 'Modular Components' that look good in a narrow sidebar AND a wide main area
Building design systems where components are decoupled from the page layout
Replacing complex JS-based resize observers with native CSS
gotchas: Container queries are relatively new; ensure your target browser supports them or use a polyfill
You must first mark the parent element as a 'container' using the `container-type` property

steps.label: Container
steps.annotation: Parent element declared as a containment context.

steps.label: Child Renders
steps.annotation: Browser measures the container's inline size.

steps.label: Apply Styles
steps.annotation: Styles applied based on container size — not viewport.

steps.label: Resize
steps.annotation: Container resizes (e.g. sidebar collapses) — styles re-evaluated automatically.

exercise.prompt: Identify the CSS property used on the parent to define it as a queryable container.
hints: It turns the element into a 'co_______'
exercise.test_code:
def check(ans): return ans.lower() == "container"
assert check("container")
print("PASS")
